paper.title_en: Finsler-Randers cosmology with anisotropic evolution and modified Chaplygin gas as a unified dark sector model
paper.abstract_en: We investigate a cosmological model based on Finsler–Randers geometry incorporating a dynamically evolving anisotropic contribution, coupled with a modified Chaplygin gas as a unified description of the dark sector. The anisotropy is modeled through a redshift-dependent function, allowing a smooth connection between an early-time FLRW-like regime and a weakly anisotropic late-time universe. The modified Friedmann equations are derived using the osculating-Riemannian approach, and the expansion dynamics are constrained using Cosmic Chronometers, DESI DR2 BAO, and DES-Dovekie supernova datasets. The model provides an acceptable fit to the datasets, with reduced chi-square values below unity. The anisotropic contribution is found to be perturbative, producing only mild deviations at low and intermediate redshifts and yielding a non-phantom effective equation of state. The deceleration parameter and the effective equation-of-state parameter confirm a smooth transition from a matter-dominated phase to late-time acceleration. The Bayesian evidence gives positive support to the Finsler–Randers model, especially after including the DES-Dovekie sample. Thus, the proposed framework remains observationally viable and provides a geometrically motivated extension in which late-time acceleration arises from the interplay between weak anisotropic corrections and a unified dark fluid.
